title: Boost Retention: - Spread It Out: Review Material in Smaller Sessions over Days Instead
subtitle: Use the Lag Effect for Learning
description:
To boost retention:
- Spread it out: Review material in smaller sessions over days instead of cramming.
- Schedule reviews: Create a spaced repetition plan.
- Take breaks: Let your brain consolidate information.

Example: Study vocabulary for 20 minutes each day instead of 2 hours in one sitting.
keywords: Use the Lag Effect for Learning

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify your current retention metrics by tracking how many items you can recall correctly in a single session. This will serve as your baseline for measuring improvement.

howTo.title: Schedule spaced review sessions
howTo.summary: Create a plan to review material in smaller sessions over several days. Aim for 20-minute sessions and space them out to enhance retention.

howTo.title: Implement active retrieval techniques
howTo.summary: During each session, focus on active retrieval methods such as flashcards or self-quizzing. This engages your memory and reinforces learning.

howTo.title: Reflect on your progress
howTo.summary: After each session, take a moment to reflect on what worked and what didn’t. Adjust your methods based on your experiences to improve future sessions.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and maintain accountability. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/spaced-recall-coach/.
